$comment: AdjudiCLAIMS Performance Baseline Thresholds — AJC-12. All values in milliseconds unless noted. Update values when infrastructure changes materially (e.g. Cloud Run tier upgrade, region change). Rationale for each threshold is in tests/performance/README.md.

chat_latency.description: Chat endpoint full round-trip: UPL classify → RAG retrieve → Claude API → output validate → response. Measured at POST /api/claims/:claimId/chat.

rationale.p50_ms: Median user experience — must feel fast for routine queries (AWE calculation, deadline lookup)
rationale.p95_ms: 95th percentile — acceptable for AI-assisted responses; Claude API introduces variance
rationale.p99_ms: 99th percentile — accounts for cold starts, large document context, and Claude API tail latency

document_processing.description: Document upload and OCR pipeline throughput. Measured from POST /api/claims/:claimId/documents (multipart upload) through Document AI processing completion.

rationale.p50_ms: Typical single-page medical report through Google Document AI
rationale.p95_ms: Multi-page reports up to 20 pages; complex layouts (EOR, medical charts)
rationale.p99_ms: Large documents (50+ pages); Document AI async processing tail
rationale.max_file_size_mb: Hard limit enforced by server; 50 MB covers all practical WC document types
rationale.max_pages_for_p95_target: p95 target only applies to documents ≤20 pages; larger docs may exceed

concurrent_users.description: 10 concurrent users over 30 seconds — simulates a morning shift start at a mid-size TPA. Measured via simultaneous requests to health and auth-gated endpoints.

rationale.p95_ms: Under 10-user concurrent load, p95 must stay under 1500 ms for non-AI endpoints
rationale.p99_ms: 3s p99 gives headroom for auth processing under burst load
rationale.max_error_rate_pct: Zero 5xx errors under 10-user load — any 500 is a reliability failure
rationale.max_p95_degradation_vs_single_user_pct: Concurrency must not degrade p95 by more than 50% vs single-user baseline

health_endpoints.description: Liveness and readiness probes — must respond fast for Cloud Run health checks.

rationale.liveness_p50_ms: GET /api/health has no DB call; sub-300ms median is easily achievable
rationale.liveness_p95_ms: Cloud Run health check timeout is typically 5s; 1s p95 gives ample margin
rationale.readiness_p50_ms: GET /api/health/db requires a Prisma ping; 500ms median allows for cold pool
rationale.readiness_p95_ms: Database cold query can take 500-800ms; 2s p95 accounts for this plus Cloud SQL wake

auth_endpoints.description: Login and session check latency. argon2 bcrypt work factor adds deliberate latency to login.

rationale.login_p50_ms: argon2id hash verification adds ~400-800ms intentionally (brute-force resistance)
rationale.login_p95_ms: Under concurrent login load, argon2 is CPU-bound; 3s p95 is acceptable
rationale.session_check_p50_ms: Unauthenticated session check returns 401 quickly without DB lookup
rationale.session_check_p95_ms: 800ms p95 covers authenticated session validation with token decryption

protected_api_endpoints.description: Standard CRUD endpoints — claims, deadlines, compliance, education. These return 401 for unauthenticated requests (fast path).

rationale.p95_ms: Auth rejection (401) should be sub-100ms; 800ms p95 accounts for authenticated requests with DB queries

upl_classifier.description: UPL query zone classifier (GREEN/YELLOW/RED) — lightweight LLM call that must complete before chat response is generated.

rationale.p95_ms: Classifier is a fast Gemini Flash call; 2s p95 accounts for cold model warm-up

peak_load.description: 50 concurrent users — peak load simulation for the full Cloud Run deployment. Validates that a single Cloud Run instance handles burst without 5xx.

rationale.health_p95_ms: 50 concurrent health requests should resolve within 3s even under CPU contention
rationale.max_5xx_errors: Zero server errors under peak load is a hard requirement for production readiness
